name: Makes 1 glass • Preparation time: 5 minutes

maca powder (see p.152) large handful of mâche or baby spinach

method: If you are able to buy young coconut meat (and more and more online retailers are offering young coconut), then it is worth doing so to try out this recipe. This is, for me, more of a dessert than a drink, but of course it still counts as a smoothie.
Makes 2 glasses • Preparation time: 10 minutes coconut and break off the lid. Pour the coconut water into a measuring cup, measuring out
Save the extra liquid to use in another recipe.
Using a tablespoon, scrape the coconut meat from the inside shell and set aside. Sieve the coconut water through a fine-meshed sieve.
Place all the ingredients into a blender. Purée until smooth.
Peel the mango and cut the mango flesh off the pit.
Using a large, heavy knife, hack around the pointed end of the
1–2 tsp coconut sugar small handful of fresh mint leaves or 2 drops mint oil
Makes 2 glasses • Preparation time: 10 minutes plus chilling
Place all the ingredients into a blender. Purée on the highest setting for 1 minute. Chill the lassi in the refrigerator for 30 minutes before serving.

This drink was developed by Linas Kesminas, a good friend of mine. He is an exceptionally gifted raw food chef, and I have learned many of my tips and tricks from him. The extraordinary, delectable taste of this drink took me by surprise—yet it is so easy to make, it had to have a place in our collection of recipes.
⁄2 tsp ground cardamom seeds pinch of grated turmeric root
